agent_reply: Updated [mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp:11) to include `conf.h` and removed the local constant definitions.

user_prompt: I made changes in `lib\AudioManager` related to the new switch `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` which switches between using bluedroid and libsbc
review these changes, implement the marked TODO items
agent_reply: - `AudioManager` now includes [conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h) so `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` is actually visible there.
- Added `-I lib/bluedroid` to [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:12), since `AudioManager` includes `bluedroid_sbc.h` directly.
Review note: I didn’t find other functional issues in the switch code after the TODOs were filled. The one practical integration issue was the missing wrapper include path, which is now fixed.
- `rg` confirms the marked Bluedroid TODOs are gone.
- `lib\bluedroid\bluedroid_sbc.c` still compiles directly with the ESP32 Xtensa compiler.
- `pio pkg pack lib\bluedroid` succeeds, though PlatformIO still prints its local `.platformio\.cache` ownership warning.
- Full `pio run` still fails on the existing ESP32 WiFi `Network.h` include problem before a complete firmware link.

user_prompt: you know how we are using `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC`? add a switch for `USE_LIBSBC_MSBC`, and if neither are used, do the "assume it's PCM" way
agent_reply: Default is now **PCM callback mode** because both switches are commented in [conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:12):
- neither defined: assume HFP callbacks are already PCM and queue/dequeue PCM directly.
I also added [lib/sbc/library.json](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/sbc/library.json:1), plus the PlatformIO include/dependency entries so `USE_LIBSBC_MSBC` actually builds.
- default PCM mode: `pio run -e theflybringuptest` succeeds
The PCM default should be the one to flash for your next voicemail test.

user_prompt: was that the right move? if one of `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` or `USE_LIBSBC_MSBC`, can you still check for a header and see if PCM is the right move? or is that not possible? Do not change the file if it is not possible
agent_reply: Yes, that was the right move. Your logs strongly said “PCM is the real payload” despite the HFP audio state saying mSBC: 240-byte callbacks line up with 120 samples of 16-bit mono PCM, while mSBC encoded frames would look like framed 57-byte-ish data with recognizable headers.

I made that runtime-detectable in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:183). If either `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` or `USE_LIBSBC_MSBC` is enabled, it now checks for an mSBC H2/header pattern before decoding. If the callback is PCM-sized and has no mSBC header, it logs that decision and queues it as PCM instead. That is possible because the header would be in the callback byte stream if ESP-IDF were handing us encoded mSBC frames.
I restored [conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:12) to the current default with both codec switches commented out.
Next run, the useful line to look for is `HFP callback payload detected as PCM...` or `encoded mSBC...`; that will tell us which branch it chose on the device.

user_prompt: put all statistics collection calls behind preprocessor `#ifdef`, expose them through `inc\conf.h`
there's a lot of `E (24465) i2s_common: i2s_channel_read(1257): The channel is not enabled` in the logs, probably because the mic is being pumped without being in mic mode? easy fix for you I think
agent_reply: - the 2-second `audio diag ...` logs in [test_btspeakerphone.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_btspeakerphone.cpp:54) are not compiled

I also addressed the `i2s_channel_read(...): The channel is not enabled` spam. `stop_i2s()` now marks the mode stopped before disabling channels and takes the same pump mutex, while `pump_mic2bt()` serializes the I2S read against that lifecycle path in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:494) and [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:832). Bluetooth outgoing-ready notification is deferred until after that lock is released.

commit_message: cleaned up diagnostics a bit, added mutex for pump functions

user_prompt: We have made a lot of progress, a lot of things are working the way I want
Walk through the documentation I wrote earlier, we might've deviated a lot. Warn me where the implementation does not match the written documentation, and tell me suggested changes
Note: I am thinking about making the device way more secure, and the `bluetooth.json` and `wifi.json` will eventually go away, so ignore things that involve that
agent_reply: 1. **The documented file-transfer/filesystem design no longer matches the code.**

The docs still say Wi-Fi provides an FTP server, the app treats onboard flash and microSD as separate disks, and LittleFS is used: [README.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/README.md:7), [file-system-and-ftp-server-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/file-system-and-ftp-server-design.md:1).
- no FTP implementation beyond a readme in [lib/FtpServer/readme.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/readme.md:1)
- a microSD-only HTTP file server in [WebServer.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:376)
- that HTTP server is only visibly initialized by the webserver test, not normal firmware startup: [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:53)
Suggested change: decide the real transfer direction now.
- If HTTP/web UI is the intended path, update README and filesystem docs from “FTP + LittleFS + two disks” to “microSD file access over HTTP/web UI” and delete or mark FTP notes as obsolete.
- If FTP and onboard flash still matter, they are implementation gaps, not documentation details.
2. **Several documented Wi-Fi and UI workflows are still scaffolding.**

The docs describe AP mode, station selection, NTP sync, cloud upload selection/progress, file viewing, and RTC setting: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:3), [wifi-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/wifi-workflow.md:9).
- selecting a configured station, AP, cloud upload, and NTP action only logs: [main.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:530)
That would avoid the docs reading like finished product behavior while these surfaces are still stubs.
3. **The call-recording UI behavior has drifted from the written UI contract.**

The docs say the user can change call volume, mute/unmute the mic, and see a 3-pixel tally border that becomes dotted when local mic is muted: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:7). The Bluetooth workflow also says the recording screen shows mute status and output volume: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:19).
- exposes push-to-talk mode switching and **speaker** mute in [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:185)

- uses a fixed 10-pixel solid red frame in [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:21) and [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:403)
- has mic mute support in `AudioManager`, but I did not find GUI wiring for it: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1307)
- has volume APIs, but not a recording-view volume control: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1231)
Suggested change: choose one UI model.
- If the device is primarily push-to-talk on internal hardware, rewrite the docs around P2T and speaker mute.
- If mic mute and volume are still required call controls, add those controls and make the tally border encode mic state as documented.
4. **Recorder performance/file-format docs are slightly ahead of current behavior.**

The file-format doc says new recordings should pre-allocate roughly 1 GB and truncate afterward: [recording-file-format.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/recording-file-format.md:7). The dual-core note also assumes pre-grown `.rec` files: [dual-core-load-distribution.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/dual-core-load-distribution.md:113).

Current code has preallocation implemented but disabled by default: [conf.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:14), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:418).

Also, the doc says packets have a header and payload; the current on-disk implementation writes a **fixed-size** `file_packet_t` for normal `.rec` packets, including a fixed payload array and `payload_length`: [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:31), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:230).
- update the format doc to explicitly say `.rec` packets are fixed-size today
- say preallocation is optional/compile-time unless you intend to enable it again
5. **The high-level audio/hardware docs overstate two areas.**

The high-level design says an external WM8960 or SGTL5000 codec may be enabled by build flag: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:9). In the current audio manager, external I2S codec enable functions are explicit “not implemented yet” stubs: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:626).

The same doc says CVSD local PCM is 8 kHz: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:31). The implementation accepts 8 kHz HFP, but `AudioFifo` upsamples 8 kHz input into its 16 kHz internal representation, and recorder packets are labeled `*_16KHZ_MONO`: [AudioFifo.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:90), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:315).
- state “external codec path planned, internal M5Stack audio path implemented”
- HFP CVSD ingress/egress can be 8 kHz
- recorder/internal FIFO normalizes to 16 kHz today

- Bluetooth connection auto-starts a call recording after entering a connected/audio-available state, matching the Bluetooth workflow: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:13), [main.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:166).

- The recording stop path can also disconnect Bluetooth: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:17), [RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp:64).

- Wi-Fi activation shuts down Bluetooth/audio/recording first, which matches the high-level design: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:17), [WifiManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:291).

- RTC-based recording naming and `.rec` output are in place: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:21), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:68).
- Top bar date/time and battery status are implemented: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:16), [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:630).

Ignoring the eventual removal of `bluetooth.json` and `wifi.json` as requested: the recording format already has security hooks, but they are placeholders right now. `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` adds nonce fields and leaves encryption/authentication as TODOs: [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:39), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:206).
Before hardening transfer/config storage, I would write one short security design doc that states:
- how keys are provisioned and recovered
- what local UI actions are allowed without trust/authentication
- whether the HTTP file surface survives at all
I did not count `bluetooth.json` or `wifi.json` mismatches in the findings. I also did not run builds or tests for this pass; this was a documentation-to-code review.

user_prompt: the other weird part: I don't remember it being slow, did something change?
agent_reply: Yes. The likely change is from the microSD fatal-error work we just added.

Now [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:401) also calls `MicroSdCard::health()` **before every recording file open**. That new health check in [MicroSdCard.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.cpp:121) does:

That free-cluster scan is the prime suspect for a multi-second delay on SdFat, especially on a larger or fuller card. `readCID()` is a deliberate card-presence probe and should be comparatively small; the free-space calculation is the part I would not want on the hot path for “tap memo, start now”.

I checked the recent committed memo path too: `RecordingView::beginMemoRecording()` and `RecordingViewCallbacks::beginMemoRecording()` have not picked up a new slow step in the committed history. Also `ENABLE_FILE_PREALLOCATION` is currently commented out in [conf.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:14), so `grow_file()` is not the reason.
- On recording start, do only the cheap missing/unreadable probe before open.
- Only if opening fails, do the slower classification work to decide whether to report “card missing/unreadable”, “card full”, or generic file-open failure.
That still keeps microSD failures fatal and gives the better error when something goes wrong, without making every memo start pay for a free-space scan.

user_prompt: I want to run `void test_sdcard()` to test performance of the recorder with encryption enabled
make sure everything is wired so that it works with encryption actually built and active
agent_reply: Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.
- installs a deterministic test master key only if no key already exists,
- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.
Wired `test_sdcard()` so it should actually test encrypted recorder performance now.
- `setup()` now calls `all_init()` before bring-up tests, so NVS/M5/gui basics exist before `Aegis` and SD setup.

- forces `AudioFileRecorder::setPurePcmMode(false)` so it does not bypass encryption.
- `AudioFileRecorder` now includes `Aegis` by relative path so PlatformIO library discovery works.
- `BtHostList.h` and `WifiManager.h` now include `conf.h`, so their security-mode structs match their `.cpp` files.
Build passed. I restored the generated `src/version.c` bump afterward.

user_prompt: the document `lib\Security\security-levels.md` is up to date and reflects what I want. `lib\Security\security-policies-specifications.md` reflects the maximum security practices but `security-levels.md` has priority.
do a clean up of mistakes when I did the switching of the `#define`
also, get rid of any code that deals with `legacy` nvs storage, we do not need to care about that until development has reached some form of release
agent_reply: Cleaned up the `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` switch fallout and removed legacy NVS migration paths.
- Removed old `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` branches and old linked-list compatibility paths from Wi-Fi/Bluetooth config code.
- Removed Wi-Fi and Bluetooth legacy NVS migration handling.
- Fixed fixed-entry structs fallout: removed `next_node` usage and cloud `name` usage.
- Level 0: `/get_cfg` and `/set_cfg` use plain JSON, cloud upload auth uses per-cloud password.
- Level 1+: config transport stays session encrypted, cloud upload auth uses `network-key`.
- FTP only starts when `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is set and `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL <= 0`.
- Boot now loads Wi-Fi/Bluetooth JSON only for level 0; higher levels load NVS directly.
- Cloud list labels now use URL instead of removed cloud name.
- `git diff --check` on the files I edited passed.
- All four got through compilation and failed only at the known link-stage IRAM0 issue: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2104 bytes`.

Global `git diff --check` still reports trailing whitespace in `.vscode/extensions.json`, `inc/conf.h`, and `lib/Security/security-levels.md`; I left those alone since they look like your existing macro/doc edit churn.
Cleaned up the `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL` switch fallout and removed legacy NVS migration paths.
- Removed old `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` branches and old linked-list compatibility paths from Wi-Fi/Bluetooth config code.
- Removed Wi-Fi and Bluetooth legacy NVS migration handling.
- Fixed fixed-entry structs fallout: removed `next_node` usage and cloud `name` usage.
- Level 0: `/get_cfg` and `/set_cfg` use plain JSON, cloud upload auth uses per-cloud password.
- Level 1+: config transport stays session encrypted, cloud upload auth uses `network-key`.
- FTP only starts when `BUILD_FTP_SERVER` is set and `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL <= 0`.
- Boot now loads Wi-Fi/Bluetooth JSON only for level 0; higher levels load NVS directly.
- Cloud list labels now use URL instead of removed cloud name.
- `git diff --check` on the files I edited passed.
- All four got through compilation and failed only at the known link-stage IRAM0 issue: `.iram0.text` overflowed by `2104 bytes`.

Global `git diff --check` still reports trailing whitespace in `.vscode/extensions.json`, `inc/conf.h`, and `lib/Security/security-levels.md`; I left those alone since they look like your existing macro/doc edit churn.
